title: Charity Majors: AI Code Is Disposable, Demands More Discipline
summary: Charity Majors argues that AI has turned the economics of code production upside down, making code generation effectively free and instant, and shifting code from treasured to disposable. This fundamental shift demands more engineering discipline as the focus moves from writing code to curating and validating it, affecting how software teams prioritize architecture, testing, and system design. Majors notes that lines of code went from being carefully curated to disposable and regenerable practically overnight, highlighting a dramatic change in developer mindset.
context: Traditionally, software development involved high costs for writing and maintaining code, making reuse and careful curation essential. With AI-assisted programming, generating large amounts of code becomes cheap and fast, reducing the marginal cost of new code. This economic shift requires engineers to invest more effort in system-level thinking and quality assurance rather than manual coding.
